ucs.title: [LLM] SilverTerrier commodity infostealer SMTP/FTP credential exfil (Agent Tesla / LokiBot / Pony)
ucs.description: Hunts the signature SilverTerrier C2 pattern: a non-mail-client process running from a user-writable path beaconing over SMTP(S)/FTP to exfiltrate stolen credentials. SilverTerrier's BEC operations rely heavily on Agent Tesla, LokiBot, AzoRult, Pony and PredatorPain, all of which embed an attacker SMTP/FTP credential and POST harvested logins directly to mailboxes the actor controls.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest_ips values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as ports from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port IN (25,465,587,21) AND All_Traffic.app!="unknown" by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.app All_Traffic.process_name host
| `drop_dm_object_name("All_Traffic")`
| search NOT app IN ("outlook.exe","thunderbird.exe","mailbird.exe","hMailServer.exe","smtp.exe","postfix*","sendmail*","exchange*")
| join type=inner src process_name [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_path
    | rename Processes.dest as src Processes.process_name as process_name Processes.parent_process_name as parent Processes.process_path as image_path
]
| where count >= 3
| eval risk_reason="non-mail process performing SMTP/FTP from user-writable path - SilverTerrier infostealer pattern"
| table firstTime lastTime host src process_name image_path parent ports dest_ips count risk_reason
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
let mailParents = dynamic(["outlook.exe","thunderbird.exe","mailbird.exe","hMailServer.exe","em_client.exe"]);
let userWritable = dynamic([@"\appdata\roaming\", @"\appdata\local\temp\", @"\programdata\", @"\users\public\"]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where RemotePort in (25, 465, 587, 21, 20)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (mailParents)
| extend ImgLower = tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
| where ImgLower has_any (userWritable)
| summarize Connections=count(), DistinctRemotes=dcount(RemoteIP), Remotes=make_set(RemoteIP,25), Ports=make_set(RemotePort,5), FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp)
  by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessParentFileName, InitiatingProcessSHA256
| where Connections >= 3
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","excel.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe")
    | project DeviceName, ChildSHA=SHA256, ChildName=FileName, OfficeParent=InitiatingProcessFileName
  ) on $left.InitiatingProcessSHA256 == $right.ChildSHA, DeviceName
| extend Verdict = iff(isnotempty(OfficeParent), "HIGH - Office/Script lineage + SMTP exfil", "MEDIUM - non-mail SMTP/FTP from user path")
| project FirstSeen, LastSeen, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessParentFileName, OfficeParent, Ports, Remotes, Connections, Verdict, InitiatingProcessSHA256
ucs.rationale: Unit 42's multi-year SilverTerrier reporting documents that >90% of their malware families (Agent Tesla, LokiBot, AzoRult, Pony, PredatorPain, ISRStealer, NanoCore, NetWire) exfiltrate to attacker-controlled mailboxes over SMTP/465/587 or FTP — a tradecraft choice tied to Nigerian BEC monetization rather than nation-state staged C2. Legitimate enterprise endpoints rarely have non-Outlook binaries

ucs.title: [LLM] SilverTerrier phishing chain: Office spawns scripting host that drops & runs binary from %AppData%
ucs.description: Catches SilverTerrier's classic delivery-to-install pivot: a weaponized invoice/PO/shipping document (often .doc, .xls, .iso, or .img) opened by the victim spawns wscript/cscript/mshta/powershell, which then writes and launches a PE under the user's profile. This 3-hop chain (Office → script host → user-path PE) is the consistent precursor to their Agent Tesla/LokiBot deployments tracked by Unit 42.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process_id Processes.process
| `drop_dm_object_name("Processes")`
| rename process_id as script_pid process_name as script_host
| join type=inner dest script_pid [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*") AND Processes.process_name IN ("*.exe","*.scr","*.pif") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_id Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_hash
    | rename Processes.parent_process_id as script_pid Processes.process_name as dropped_bin Processes.process_path as dropped_path Processes.process_hash as dropped_hash Processes.dest as dest
]
| eval kill_chain="OfficeApp -> ".script_host." -> ".dropped_bin
| table firstTime lastTime dest user parent_process_name script_host script_pid dropped_bin dropped_path dropped_hash kill_chain
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
let officeApps = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","acrord32.exe"]);
let scriptHosts = dynamic(["wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe"]);
let userPaths = dynamic([@"\appdata\roaming\", @"\appdata\local\temp\", @"\users\public\", @"\programdata\"]);
let scriptStage =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (officeApps)
    | where FileName in~ (scriptHosts)
    | project ScriptTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, OfficeParent=InitiatingProcessFileName, ScriptHost=FileName, ScriptPid=ProcessId, ScriptCmd=ProcessCommandLine, AccountName;
let dropStage =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".scr" or FileName endswith ".pif"
    | extend ImgLower = tolower(FolderPath)
    | where ImgLower has_any (userPaths)
    | project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DroppedBin=FileName, DroppedPath=FolderPath, DroppedSHA=SHA256, DropParentPid=InitiatingProcessId, DropParentName=InitiatingProcessFileName;
scriptStage
| join kind=inner (dropStage) on DeviceId
| where DropParentPid == ScriptPid
| where DropTime between (ScriptTime .. ScriptTime + 10m)
| extend Chain = strcat(OfficeParent, " -> ", ScriptHost, " -> ", DroppedBin)
| project ScriptTime, DropTime, DeviceName, AccountName, Chain, OfficeParent, ScriptHost, ScriptCmd, DroppedBin, DroppedPath, DroppedSHA
| join kind=leftouter (
    EmailAttachmentInfo
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | project EmailTime=Timestamp, NetworkMessageId, AttachmentSHA=SHA256, FileName, SenderFromAddress
  ) on $left.DroppedSHA == $right.AttachmentSHA
| extend Severity = iff(isnotempty(SenderFromAddress), "CRITICAL - dropped binary matches inbound email attachment", "HIGH - Office->Script->User-path PE chain")
| order by ScriptTime desc
ucs.rationale: SilverTerrier (G0083) is a Nigerian BEC/419-evolved cluster whose entire economic model depends on commodity-RAT delivery via invoice/shipping/PO themed phishing — not on living-off-the-land or zero-days. Unit 42 sample analyses repeatedly show the same 3-stage local execution graph: Office → script interpreter → user-writable PE within seconds. Joining the dropped-binary SHA256 back to EmailAttac
